messages.subject: [R-jobs] Head of Department of Applied Statistics and Epidemiology - Institute of Cancer Epidemiology, Copenhagen, Denmark
messages.subject_clean: [R-jobs] Head of Department of Applied Statistics and Epidemiology - Institute of Cancer Epidemiology, Copenhagen, Denmark
messages.body_plain:
Head of Department of Applied Statistics and Epidemiology - Institute of Cancer Epidemiology, Copenhagen, Denmark





The Institute

Population-based cancer research in the Nordic countries is in the international forefront.  Since the foundation of the Danish Cancer Registry in 1942 the Institute of Cancer Epidemiology has played a significant role in this research area. The Institute is looking for a visionary leader to take charge of our Department of Applied Statistics and Epidemiology.



The position

As Head of Department you will take part in the leadership of the Institute, at present composed by six Heads of Department and one Head of Programme. Supervision of currently five statisticians and of PhD-students and young researchers at the Institute is required.  Ability to collaborate with individual researchers and research groups within and outside our institute, including clinicians and laboratory researchers at treatment centres, is necessary.



Required qualifications

Your research background should be on a postdoctoral level, preferably on a professor level.

You must hold extensive knowledge in theoretical statistics and also have attained substantial practical experience in the area of applied statistics.  Experience with epidemiological research, including research based on use of register information and data obtained directly from study subjects, will be an asset.  Ability to attract research funding is an advantage.



Your qualifications will be assessed by an evaluation committee consisting of one internal and two external scientists.  Qualified candidates will be invited for an interview with an appointments committee composed by the Director of the Institute and department staff, who will then select the successful candidate.



We offer

We offer a professionally stimulating job in a friendly and personal atmosphere, with the prospect of career development. Salary will be provided according to the internal rules within the Danish Cancer Society and reflecting current agreements between the Danish Cancer Society and the relevant union organizations.



The Danish Cancer Society is located in Copenhagen, close to Copenhagen City. We have plenty of room for social interactions and activities, and have several benefits for the personnel, such as canteen and discount on fitness. The everyday working language of the department is English and the working place is off course smoke-free.



Further information

Further information about the position can be obtained from the Director of the Institute of Cancer Epidemiology, Dr. J?rgen H. Olsen, tel. +45 3525 7654, e-mail: jorgen at cancer.dk<mailto:jorgen at cancer.dk>.

You can read more about the Institute at www.cancer.dk/epi<http://www.cancer.dk/epi>.



Application and closing date for applications Closing date is 24 June 2010. The application should be written in English and include a summary of your research experience, research interests, and a complete CV, including a review of your education and a full list of publications.  Please indicate which publications you wish to have included in the assessment.



Please send your application in four copies, marked 'Head of Statistics', to



Danish Cancer Society

HR Department

Strandboulevarden 49

DK-2100 Copenhagen

Denmark



Please note that you can not apply for the position at the Danish Cancer Society's homepage (www.cancer.dk<http://www.cancer.dk>). Please send your application to the postal address mentioned above.


----------------------------------------------



	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-jobs] ORAU/ORISE Job Opening - Scientific Programmer/Analyst 2
messages.body_plain:
Oak Ridge Associated Universities is seeking a Scientific
Programmer/Analyst 2 to support the Occupational Exposure and Worker
Health (OEWH) Beryllium Laboratory operation, including a redesign of
the current system and incorporation of R routines into the LPT
analysis, and also to support the data analysis in the Department of
Energy (DOE) Beryllium Registry.  This includes expansion of an existing
R package that analyzes exposure data, producing several statistical
measures.  This position will also support other MS SQL applications
within OEWH. Visit
http://www.orau.org/careers/description.aspx?JobId=957 for a full job
description, job requirements and to apply online. 

 

 

LaShanda Miller

Employment Specialist

Oak Ridge Associated Universities

865/576-3166

lashanda.miller at orau.org

 


	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-jobs] Immediate Consulting Opportunity: Health Care Informatics Statistical Programmer and Research Support Analyst
messages.subject_clean: [R-jobs] Immediate Consulting Opportunity: Health Care Informatics Statistical Programmer and Research Support Analyst
messages.body_plain:
We are looking to add experts to our fast growing managed service practice
that provides access to a research platform and selected commercial medical
observational data bases. Preferred locations are Boston and Philadelphia

Roles and Responsibilities:
Assist clients in the use of the research platform and data, providing
technical support to client researchers who want to use the research
platform and data bases to develop and run statistical analysis programs.
Assist client statisticians and programmers in optimizing their programs
using a wide range of data mining and statistical techniques.

Ideal Skill Requirements:
At  least three years of experience working with a Life Sciences or Health
Care organization research?team using observational as well as clinical data
and 3 ? 8 years in using SAS and/or R to analyze large claims and electronic
medical record data bases for safety and /or outcomes and economics
research. 
? Experience with Base SAS, SAS/STAT, and R is also required
? Helpful to have experience with SAS/GRAPH, SAS Visual Data Discovery,
Oracle and Postgree SQL as well as UNIX scripting 
? Helpful to have experience with one or more of the following data bases:
Thomson Reuters Mediscan, GE, i3 and GPRD data
? Knowledge of the common data models and methods developed by the
Observational Medical Outcomes Partnership is a plus.
? Equivalent experience working for a CRO or university is acceptable

Bill Tomassini
Partner
william.tomassini at mcmcons.com
www.mcmcons.com
Phone: 484-678-9781
Fax: 610-631-3231
